mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of t-1mo indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% as of t-1mo indicates elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC of UNRATE as predictor falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, indicating weakening residential demand that signals broader economic contraction and negative equity market sentiment over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890k units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 99.0 index level while simultaneously declining month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness that constrains corporate earnings growth and depresses equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4965 as of t-1mo near critical threshold
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO as predictor of 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously over a 2-month period, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421.4B and HOUST at 890k as of t-1mo and t-2mo
failure_condition: The joint signal (both RSAFS and HOUST declining) produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains elevated above 4.0%, signaling labor market slack that constrains wage growth and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135.0M and UNRATE at 7.7% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration with slack
failure_condition: The joint condition (weak payroll growth AND elevated unemployment) produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, indicating real rates are negative and the Fed is behind the curve. This regime produces positive equity returns over 5 trading days as markets price in asset reflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where real rates are negative and inflation accelerating, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
